context: Tests whether the agent implements proper error handling in a Ruby service object: rescuing specific exception types, logging errors, returning structured error hashes, validating inputs early, and handling partial failures gracefully.

checklist.name: Early input validation
checklist.description: The service raises (or returns an error) before reaching business logic when required input is missing or invalid — validation happens at the start of the `call` method

checklist.name: Specific rescue clauses
checklist.description: The `call` method rescues at least one specific exception class (e.g. `ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid`, `ArgumentError`) rather than only `StandardError`

checklist.name: StandardError rescue
checklist.description: A `rescue StandardError` (or equivalent catch-all) is present as a fallback to prevent any exception escaping

checklist.name: Error logged
checklist.description: Each rescue block calls a logging method (e.g. `log_error`, `Rails.logger.error`, or similar) — NOT just silently rescuing

checklist.name: Error hash returned
checklist.description: Each rescue block returns a hash with `success: false` (not re-raises the exception)

checklist.name: No exception leakage
checklist.description: No rescue block calls `raise` or `raise e` — exceptions are fully absorbed and converted to error hashes

checklist.name: Partial success response
checklist.description: When some items succeed and others fail, the response includes BOTH successful results AND failure details in the same response hash (partial success pattern)

checklist.name: Frozen string literal
checklist.description: Service file begins with `# frozen_string_literal: true`

checklist.name: Response format consistency
checklist.description: All return paths (success, partial success, error) use the `{ success: ..., response: { ... } }` structure

checklist.name: Backtrace logged for unexpected errors
checklist.description: The catch-all `rescue StandardError` logs a backtrace (e.g. passes `include_backtrace: true` or logs `e.backtrace`)
